mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate earnings revisions upward and pushing equity prices higher over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0 over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts and revolving consumer credit signals broad-based demand expansion. This joint signal compresses credit spreads and lifts equity risk premiums, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns in at least 60% of out-of-sample windows, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a coefficient with p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and healthy supply-side momentum. This operational leverage boosts profit margins, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns turn negative when INDPRO prints above its 12-month moving average for three consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index breaches 238.0, inflation expectations become unanchored. Central banks respond by hiking policy rates, which increases the equity risk premium and compresses valuation multiples, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of observations where CPIAUCSL crosses above 238.0, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 45% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth directly expands corporate revenue bases. This earnings momentum translates into positive S&P 500 price appreciation over the subsequent week as analysts upgrade quarterly forecasts.
failure_condition: The rolling 12-month Spearman correlation between quarterly GDP growth surprises and 5-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising nonfarm payrolls and a stable unemployment rate confirms sustainable labor demand. This dual confirmation reduces recession probabilities, lowering equity volatility and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction signal produces negative 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of out-of-sample periods, or the joint predictive power vanishes when controlling for VIX levels.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime where the Fed is actively hiking rates while inflation remains sticky, equity valuations face dual compression from higher discount rates and margin pressure. This mechanism drives negative S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.10 in a single month
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the hypothesis fails to outperform a random baseline in a 5-year walk-forward test.
